file: Tips on Taking a Video Recording of an Oral History and why it needs to be done NOW!
brief: The idea of taking down our old history is to actually get the  person who may be sick, may be passing away, may be old, may be beginning to suffer dementia or it could just be  somebody young enough who actually has studied the family history. For example, Grandma used to tell me about Dolly Underwood and how they worked in a department  store in the 1920s. There is some really great websites, as I said, organized in a situation where you can be pertaining to figuring out some ideas, jot down some notes. What I find really interesting in today's world is that everything we do eat sleep for most part, especially  millennials, goes on some form of social media, TikTok, Facebook, etc.
sourceTitle: Tips on Taking a Video Recording of an Oral History and why it needs to be done NOW!
license: Public YouTube video processed transiently. Bonfyre retained only derived artifacts.
sourceCopy: Source: History with McCaskey Heritage Stories  on YouTube. Bonfyre downloaded, transcribed, and deleted the media. Only the transcript, brief, and proof remain.
whyItMatters: Family recordings become preserved, searchable oral histories — stories that would otherwise fade with time.
searchIntro: Search across the family history records to see how Bonfyre connects related content from different sources.

file: A MORE HEARTWARMING  WAY OF RECORDING FAMILY MEMORIES
brief: This Grandma's method is a little more comforting  and a little more heart-filled. (gentle music)  - Joy watched her grandmother work the needle and thread,  but there was something else happening here  that Joy could not see nor understand. A quilt may be somebody else's story,  but it makes her feel awful to think someone through a way  another grandmother's hard work. I met one woman who is preserving a way of life  and teaching others how to pass along stories  through the generations.
sourceTitle: A MORE HEARTWARMING  WAY OF RECORDING FAMILY MEMORIES
